cover.title: Why Is My Toilet Gurgling?
cover.subtitle: Answer to see if your clog is DIY'able or if you need to call a professional.
cover.description:
That bubbling sound usually means air is getting pulled through the drain somewhere it should not be.

Sometimes it is a simple clog. Sometimes it means the problem is deeper in the line.
cover.image_prompt: A clean, modern bathroom scene focused on a white toilet with subtle bubbling water in the bowl, suggesting trapped air or a drain issue. The bathroom should look realistic, bright, and slightly tense but not gross. Add faint motion/ripple detail in the toilet water, with no sewage, mess, or horror styling. Professional home-service blog image, natural light, crisp detail, trustworthy and practical.
cover.seo_description: A quick diagnostic quiz for homeowners with a gurgling toilet. Find out whether the likely cause is a shallow clog, blocked sewer line, or plumbing vent issue.

questions.title: First, is your home on city sewer or septic?
questions.subtitle: This keeps us from giving you the wrong next step.

options.label: Septic system
options.tooltip: A private septic system may need a septic company instead of a city sewer drain cleaning appointment.

questions.title: What floor is it on?
questions.subtitle: Lower fixtures usually show deeper drain problems first.

questions.title: When does the toilet gurgle?
questions.subtitle: The timing tells us where the problem may be hiding.

options.label: When I flush a different toilet
When I run water in my shower/sink/tub/washer
Randomly and it smells like sewer
A nearby tub, shower, or sink drains slowly
Several drains around the house are slow
Water backs up in a shower, tub, or floor drain
Wet yard, septic smell outside, or septic alarm
options.tooltip: If you are on a septic system, this may need a septic company instead of normal drain cleaning.

options.label: Flushing it
options.tooltip: Do not keep flushing a gurgling toilet. It can turn a partial clog into a backup.

options.label: Chemical drain cleaner
options.tooltip: Chemical drain cleaners can be harsh on pipes and usually do not fix vent or sewer line problems.

options.label: Checked septic tank
options.tooltip: If the septic tank or alarm is involved, a septic company may be the right first call.

questions.title: Want the likely cause?
questions.subtitle: Enter your name, email, and ZIP code to see the result.

questions.title: Want Mother to take a look?
questions.subtitle: You're in our service area. Share your phone and address and we can follow up.

results.title: This Looks Like a Shallow Clog
results.message:
Your answers point toward a clog close to the toilet or nearby drain. This is the most DIY'able result.

You can try a hardware store toilet auger or drain snake if you are comfortable. If the clog keeps coming back, or if plunging and a snake do not clear it, hiring a drain cleaning company is the safer next step.
results.tie_breaker_reason: Your answers are balanced, so we recommend starting with the simplest likely cause: a shallow clog close to the drain.

in_area.message: Mother serves your ZIP code and can help with drain cleaning if you want a pro to clear it.

out_of_area.message: Mother is not serving your ZIP code yet, so we cannot book this through our team right now.
out_of_area.advice: Your result is still useful. Try a toilet auger if this is the only issue, or ask a local drain cleaning company for help if it keeps coming back.

results.title: This Looks Like a Blocked Sewer Line
results.message:
Your answers point toward a deeper blockage, especially if multiple drains are involved or water is backing up in a shower, tub, or floor drain.

This is usually not a DIY solution. You will likely need sewer cleaning services, and a camera inspection may be needed before anyone recommends hydro jetting or repair.
results.html: Next step: learn about sewer line inspection and repair.
results.tie_breaker_reason: Your answers are balanced, so we recommend treating it as a deeper sewer line issue when lower fixtures, multiple drains, or backups are involved.

in_area.message: Mother serves your ZIP code and can help diagnose a deeper sewer line blockage.

out_of_area.message: Mother is not serving your ZIP code yet, so we cannot book this through our team right now.
out_of_area.advice: Your result is still useful. Ask a local plumber for sewer cleaning and camera inspection before approving major repair work.

results.title: This May Be a Vent Issue
results.message:
Your answers point toward a plumbing vent problem. Vent issues can make a toilet gurgle because air is being pulled through the drain system in the wrong place.

This is usually not a DIY solution. A plumber should check the venting path and roof vent before anyone assumes the toilet itself is the problem.
results.html: Learn more: Sewer vent problems, cleaning, and repairs.
results.tie_breaker_reason: Your answers are balanced, so we recommend checking venting when the gurgle happens without flushing, when other fixtures run, or when sewer smell is present.

in_area.message: Mother serves your ZIP code and can inspect the drain and venting path.

out_of_area.message: Mother is not serving your ZIP code yet, so we cannot book this through our team right now.
out_of_area.advice: Your result is still useful. Ask a local plumber to check venting and avoid climbing onto the roof yourself.

result_copy.disclaimer: This quiz is educational and does not replace an on-site plumbing diagnosis.
